verification_gate: Buffer flush is ordered, idempotent, restartable, and observable; AgentDB identities and learning state are portable and witness-bound; neither store can silently become competing macro state.

clause_1_buffer_flush.ordered: monotonic per-buffer sequence number assigned at enqueue; envctl drains strictly ascending so parser/operation order is preserved into PostgreSQL (buffer_flush_protocol.ordered)
clause_1_buffer_flush.idempotent: content-addressed identity (SHA-256 blob for REDB-RT-01 ingestion; per-operation idempotency key for REDB-RT-03 outbox; BLAKE3 model-bound key for REDB-RT-02 cache) against a commit-once PostgreSQL destination — a crash between apply-and-ack cannot duplicate a durable row
clause_1_buffer_flush.restartable: redb single-file ACID + MVCC re-open recovers last committed buffer state; flush worker resumes from last acked sequence via a resume skip-set; unacked entries replay in order
clause_1_buffer_flush.observable: buffer occupancy / outbox depth, drain / replay lag, per-entry flush receipt, poison-record count, cache hit/miss ratio, backpressure / capacity-remaining, scratchpad write latency

clause_2_agentdb_portable_and_witness_bo.portable: .rvf is a self-contained single-file cognitive container, hot-swappable across any ruvllm node hosting the same frozen base model, regenerable from base model + history; 50+ agents = 50 portable .rvf files on one base-model footprint
clause_2_agentdb_portable_and_witness_bo.witness_bound: AGENTDB-05 Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) + Causal Graph Verification (GNN causal edges) root every promoted fact/decision in a PostgreSQL source vector; unrooted (hallucinated) claims are rejected; AGENTDB-06 identity is anchored to that chain

clause_3_no_competing_macro_state.redb_side: every one of the 6 redb runtime roles carries a non_competition field: transient/derived, forward-only flush, no second durable writer; REDB-RT-05 geometry engine has NO SQL / secondary index / native vector logic so it cannot own semantic authority
clause_3_no_competing_macro_state.agentdb_side: every .rvf entity has macro_authority 'none'; only witness-verified/curated states flow one-way to PostgreSQL; the container is never canonical
clause_3_no_competing_macro_state.no_silent_reclassification: PGREDB-001 (CodeDB source_blobs) and PGREDB-002 (envctl migration_db) are DURABLE-today uses referenced-not-reclassified — they migrate only via STORE-001-C verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, neither silently promoted to transient nor frozen as durable
clause_3_no_competing_macro_state.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL (inherited STORE-001 invariant 0); forward-only sync, no dual-write (inherited invariant 1)

gate_result.store_001_alignment: POSTGRES-006 defines only the runtime mechanics of STORE-001-B (redb transient tier) and STORE-001-D (AgentDB cognition); it inherits STORE-001's single-durable-writer and forward-only invariants and does not re-decide any macro-state owner
open_dependencies: ordered/idempotent redb->PostgreSQL outbox protocol DEFINED not yet built (PGREDB-005)
NotebookLM 'nu_plugin buffers into redb' framing contradicts current code (PGREDB-006); intended buffer is a build target
live blobstore_parity.rs green run vs disposable PostgreSQL is the PGREDB-001 cutover proof (governed by STORE-001-C)
gate_result.no_live_mutation: no redb file, no .rvf file, no PostgreSQL row, and no model was written, trained, flushed, or migrated

proof_summary: POSTGRES-006 defines the runtime boundaries of the two non-canonical tiers STORE-001 already assigned, without duplicating PostgreSQL macro authority. generated/runtime_store_boundary_plan.json enumerates six redb transient runtime roles — REDB-RT-01 high-frequency ingestion buffer, REDB-RT-02 BLAKE3 embedding deduplication cache, REDB-RT-03 application-level WAL/outbox, REDB-RT-04 agent scratchpad, REDB-RT-05 stateless f32 geometry engine, and REDB-RT-06 read-only UDS UI view — each assigned exactly once with owner, lifetime, identity, forward-only sync edge, crash recovery, and an explicit non-competition guarantee. Gate clause 1 is satisfied by the buffer_flush_protocol: flush is ordered (monotonic per-buffer sequence drained ascending), idempotent (SHA-256 blob / per-operation idempotency-key / BLAKE3 model-bound cache key against a commit-once PostgreSQL destination), restartable (redb ACID re-open + resume skip-set replays unacked entries in order), and observable (depth, replay lag, per-entry receipts, poison-record count, hit/miss ratio). Gate clause 2 is satisfied by agentdb_cognition: all six .rvf entities (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrix, FastGRNN routing graph, learning traces, witness facts, agent identity/lifecycle) are portable (self-contained single-file container hot-swappable across ruvllm nodes and regenerable from base model + history) and witness-bound (SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chains + Causal Graph Verification root every promoted fact in a PostgreSQL source vector, rejecting hallucinations). Gate clause 3 is satisfied because every redb role and every .rvf entity is non-authoritative and flushes forward-only, and the two DURABLE-today redb uses (PGREDB-001 CodeDB source_blobs, PGREDB-002 envctl migration_db) are referenced-not-reclassified — they move to PostgreSQL only via STORE-001-C's verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, so neither store is silently promoted to durable nor silently demoted to transient, and PostgreSQL remains the single durable writer per the inherited STORE-001 invariants. Every assignment traces to the Architecture Blueprint (redb geometry engine / scratchpad / WAL / dedup cache; .rvf cognitive container; witness chains), the Architecture Context Matrix (PostgreSQL+RuVector = Canonical state, redb = Temporary/derived state), STORE-001 (store_relationship_decision.json), and the pgauth_redb_usage_inventory (PGREDB-001..007). No product execution, model training, or live redb/.rvf/PostgreSQL mutation was performed.
